name: Los Jorrios Trail (PR-BI 120)
name_original: Jorrios y Armañón desde Basinagre — circular trailrunning por el Parque Natural de Armañón
description: Jog the difficult 8.6-mile Los Jorrios Trail (PR-BI 120) in Armañón Natural Park, gaining 2555 feet with panoramic views and historical site
region_intro: Jogging around Trucios-Turtzioz offers diverse terrain in the western part of Bizkaia, Spain. The region is characterized by its mountainous landscape, including prominent peaks within Armañón Natural Park, and features like the Agüera River. Joggers can explore a mix of challenging hill routes, serene riverside paths, and lush forests. This varied environment provides options for different fitness levels and preferences. Best jogging routes around Trucios-Turtzioz The most popular jogging route is Los Jorrios Trail (PR-BI 120), a 8.6 miles (13.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 7 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses mountainous terrain within Armañón Natural Park. Another top favourite among local runners is Peak of the Snows – trail run from The Bridge, a difficult 8.0 miles (12.9 km) path. This route offers significant elevation gain and panoramic views across the landscape. Local runners also love the Pico de las Nieves loop from Guriezo, a 20.1 miles (32.3 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 11 minutes. Jogging around Trucios-Turtzioz is defined by mountainous terrain, the Agüera River, and extensive forests, with routes ranging from easy riverside paths to challenging mountain ascents. The network of running trails Trucios-Turtzioz offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Trucios-Turtzioz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 60 reviews. More than 1900 runners have used komoot to explore Trucios-Turtzioz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Trucios-Turtzioz?
A: Trucios-Turtzioz offers a wide selection of over 50 running routes. These range from easy paths suitable for a leisurely jog to challenging mountain ascents, catering to various fitness levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Trucios-Turtzioz?
A: The region boasts diverse terrain. You'll find challenging mountainous landscapes, particularly within the &lt;strong&gt;Armañón Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, offering routes with significant elevation gains. For gentler runs, there are serene paths along the &lt;strong&gt;Agüera River&lt;/strong&gt;, and the &lt;strong&gt;Itsaslur Coastal Greenway&lt;/strong&gt; provides invigorating coastal views, though some sections may include stairs.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Trucios-Turtzioz has several easy jogging routes, with at least 3 specifically categorized as easy. These typically follow flatter terrain, such as paths along the Agüera River, providing a tranquil setting for a less strenuous run or a family outing.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Trucios-Turtzioz?
A: The running trails in Trucios-Turtzioz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 60 reviews. Runners frequently praise the varied terrain, from challenging mountain trails to serene riverside paths, and the stunning natural beauty of the region, including the lush forests and panoramic viewpoints.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, the region offers several circular running routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31859907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de las Nieves loop from Guriezo&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 20.1-mile trail that takes you through mountainous terrain and returns to your starting point.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the trails in Trucios-Turtzioz?
A: Many outdoor areas in Trucios-Turtzioz, including trails within the &lt;strong&gt;Armañón Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural parks, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. Check local signage for specific rules.

Q: Are there jogging routes with scenic viewpoints or natural attractions like waterfalls?
A: Absolutely! Many routes offer spectacular vistas, especially those traversing the mountainous terrain of Armañón Natural Park. You can also find the refreshing &lt;strong&gt;Mena River Waterfall&lt;/strong&gt; near the town center, which makes for a scenic stop during a run. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/644111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Itsaslur Coastal Greenway&lt;/a&gt; also provides impressive views of cliffs and the sea.

Q: What historical or cultural sights can I see while jogging in the region?
A: Jogging routes can incorporate stops at various points of interest. You might encounter historical buildings like the &lt;strong&gt;San Juan Bautista Parish Church&lt;/strong&gt; or the &lt;strong&gt;Church of San Pedro de Romaña&lt;/strong&gt;. The &lt;strong&gt;Ermita Virgen Blanca&lt;/strong&gt; offers unparalleled panoramic views, and the &lt;strong&gt;La Era Tower&lt;/strong&gt; can be admired along the Agüera River. For more specific highlights, consider exploring &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/154441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Roque Hermitage on Mount Kolitza&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4072442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of the Snows&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there options for challenging trail runs in Trucios-Turtzioz?
A: Yes, Trucios-Turtzioz is an excellent destination for challenging trail runs, with 23 routes categorized as difficult. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058620087/los-jorrios-trail-pr-bi-120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Jorrios Trail (PR-BI 120)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999313417/peak-of-the-snows-trail-run-from-the-bridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peak of the Snows – trail run from The Bridge&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains and traverse rugged mountainous terrain, ideal for experienced runners seeking demanding workouts.

Q: Are there parking facilities near the trailheads in Trucios-Turtzioz?
A: While specific parking details vary by trailhead, many popular starting points for running routes in Trucios-Turtzioz, especially those leading into &lt;strong&gt;Armañón Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; or other well-known areas, typically have designated parking areas or roadside spots. It's recommended to check route descriptions for specific parking information.

Q: When is the best time of year for running in Trucios-Turtzioz?
A: Trucios-Turtzioz offers good running conditions throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. Summer can be warm, but higher elevation routes offer cooler air. Winter runs are possible, especially on lower-lying paths, but mountain trails may require more preparation due to weather conditions.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the running routes in Trucios-Turtzioz?
A: Yes, Trucios-Turtzioz and its surrounding villages offer various cafes, restaurants, and pubs where you can refuel after your run. Many are located in the town center or along main roads, providing opportunities to enjoy local cuisine and refreshments.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Los Jorrios Trail (PR-BI 120)?
faqs.answer: The estimated time to complete the circular route depends on your activity. For jogging, komoot suggests around 2 hours and 7 minutes. If you're hiking, expect it to take approximately 4 hours and 40 minutes due to its challenging nature and varied terrain.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty and terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered difficult for jogging and 'medium-high' difficulty for hiking. You'll encounter varied terrain, including lush meadows, dense woodlands, and unique karstic landscapes. Some sections feature uneven ground, so sturdy footwear is highly recommended. The route involves a persistent, gradual ascent and descent, with an elevation gain of about 778 meters.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail offers stunning panoramic views and takes you to two prominent peaks within the Armañón Natural Park: the Jorrios Summit and the Armañón Summit. You'll also discover historical elements like ancient paths, old stone structures, palaces, tower houses, and ancient mills, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for the Los Jorrios Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Trucios-Turtzioz. While specific parking details aren't provided, the route is circular and well-marked with panels indicating distances and directions. It's advisable to check local resources for the most convenient starting points and parking areas, often found near Basinagre.

faqs.question: Is the Los Jorrios Trail (PR-BI 120) suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' classification for jogging and 'medium-high' difficulty for hiking, with significant elevation gain and varied terrain, this trail is generally not recommended for beginners. It requires a good level of physical fitness and experience with demanding mountain routes.

faqs.question: Is the Los Jorrios Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through the Armañón Natural Park. While dogs are generally allowed in natural parks, it's always best to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety on challenging terrain. Be mindful of any specific park regulations regarding pets.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Los Jorrios Trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally ideal for visiting, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. However, be aware that from March 1 to September 15, access between Basinagre and Campapedreo may be restricted for flora and fauna conservation. Always check local conditions before planning your trip.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Armañón Natural Park or the trail itself?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Armañón Natural Park or to hike/jog the Los Jorrios Trail (PR-BI 120). However, it's always a good idea to check the official website of the Armañón Natural Park for any updated regulations or seasonal restrictions before your visit.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plants might I see on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses lush forests and diverse landscapes, making it home to various flora and fauna. You might spot different bird species, and the natural beauty includes unique karstic formations and holm oak groves. The conservation restriction during certain months highlights the ecological importance of the area.

faqs.question: Does the Los Jorrios Trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the "Los Jorrios ibilbidea" (96% of the route), "Bizkaiko bira" (62%), and sections of the "Senda de los Jorrios (PR-BI 120)" (32% and 9%). There's also a smaller segment of "Senda de los Jorrios (PR-BI 120) (Cerrado del 1 de marzo al 15 de septiembre)" (4%).

faqs.question: Is there a specific section of the trail I should be aware of?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's important to note that access between Basinagre and Campapedreo on the "Senda de los Jorrios (PR-BI 120)" may be restricted from March 1 to September 15. This measure is in place for flora and fauna conservation. Always check for current advisories before heading out, especially during these months.
